description: Dandified YUM (DNF): The modern package manager used by RPM-based Linux distributions like RedHat, CentOS, Fedora, and AlmaLinux, fully replacing the legacy 'yum'.

commands.explanation: Checks if there are package upgrades available for your system without actually installing them.
Installs the latest versions of all packages currently installed on the system, auto-confirming.
Retrieves and installs the target package along with needed dependencies from enabled repositories.
Removes the specified package. Unlike dpkg/apt, DNF also removes previously installed unused dependencies triggered by this package.
Lists AppStream modules. This displays multiple versions of software (like Node.js or PostgreSQL) available to be switched into streams.
Installs a specific stream/version of a package from the AppStream repository.
Shows the transaction history of installing, upgrading, or removing packages.
Rolls back a specific previous DNF transaction (e.g., completely reversing a package installation).
Finds out which specific package provides a particular file or command executable on the system.
Cleans out cached package data, metadata, and expired headers to force a fresh repository resync.
